primary_accounting_issue: Identification of the IFRS hedge accounting models potentially available in consolidated financial statements for foreign currency risk arising from an intragroup dividend-related receivable

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which types of hedged items are eligible for hedge accounting and when intragroup foreign currency exposures can qualify in consolidated financial statements.

This paragraph directly governs whether exchange differences on intragroup monetary items remain in consolidated financial statements, which is central to identifying the relevant hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: This Interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge accounting model for a net investment in a foreign operation and how foreign currency risk is designated within a group.
These paragraphs distinguish whether exchange differences on monetary items are recognised in profit or loss or in other comprehensive income, which informs the hedge-accounting model landscape.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs deal mainly with translation mechanics and disclosures rather than identifying peer hedge accounting approaches for the issue.
These sections address scope, derecognition, fair value option and general measurement or presentation, not the identification of peer hedge accounting models for this issue.
These paragraphs concern classification, distributions and offsetting rather than the hedge accounting model selection for foreign currency risk in consolidation.
This Interpretation addresses non-cash distributions to owners, not hedge accounting for intragroup foreign currency exposures.
These are reduced-disclosure requirements and do not determine the peer accounting approaches for the hedging issue.
These paragraphs provide disclosures for financial instruments and designated liabilities, not the underlying hedge accounting approach identification.
These disclosures for interests in other entities are not relevant to identifying peer hedge accounting models for the issue.
This Interpretation addresses member shares and liability-versus-equity classification, not foreign currency hedge accounting.
These cash flow statement presentation and disclosure requirements do not identify the applicable hedge accounting models.
This interpretation concerns tax status changes and is unrelated to the hedge accounting issue.
This practice statement is unrelated to hedge accounting model identification.
This Interpretation concerns pension surplus and minimum funding requirements, not the issue raised.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for a recognised foreign-currency-denominated monetary item
Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in a forecast transaction
Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a recognised asset or liability as a distinct eligible hedged item category, and IAS 21 indicates that exchange differences on certain intragroup monetary items remain visible in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a forecast transaction as a separate top-level hedged item category, including certain highly probable intragroup foreign currency transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedging as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk within a group.
